purpose: Core assignment demonstration (ex05 section 5.3): the same class of oversized model that fails via direct/Ollama loading succeeds via AirLLM's layer-by-layer streaming, at the cost of extreme latency.
prompt: Explain in one short sentence what virtual memory is.

prior_attempt_note: First attempt (before fix) failed with 'Torch not compiled with CUDA enabled' because airllm.AutoModel defaults to device='cuda:0'. Fixed by passing device='cpu' explicitly (src/local_llm_bench/services/airllm_service.py). Model shard splitting (40 layers, ~28GB download) took ~27 minutes on the first run and was cached under data/airllm_cache/ for this successful re-run.

comparison_to_baseline.baseline_result: failed in 6.28s: unable to allocate CPU buffer (~19.2GB requested vs ~7.65GB RAM available)
comparison_to_baseline.contrast: AirLLM succeeded on a 14B model using only 903MB peak RAM (vs tens of GB required for direct loading), at the cost of ~23 minutes wall time for 30 tokens (0.023 tokens/sec) — a textbook memory-for-latency trade-off.
